content:
My name is Katharine (Rin) Tena, and I am an aspiring game designer currently studying at the University of Pittsburgh. I am passionate about making games that provide a unique and compelling experience to their users! Aside from my professional development, my interests include traditional Filipino dancing (Tinikling), rock climbing, and painting!
View ResumeView Portfolio

PlayOverview#Red Debt Redemption is a shooting game where the player must make choices and build skills to overcome their debt, which appear in the form of various monsters. The player makes decisions about their in-game life choices, which in turn effect the amount of debt they must pay off later.
Submission for SheInnovates Hackathon 2026, which took place over the course of 2 days. Executed menu screen, level selection, and writing. Group worked in Godot, a new platform for every group member. The game is a top down shooter with the goal of teaching college students how to manage their debts.
Problem#Our main problem was that Godot was a new system for all of us, and we sought out to create a working game to become familiar with the application. We approached this by having the gameplay and scenes do familiar actions such as movement and scene switching. By relating Godot to systems we already knew such as Unity, we were better able to grasp how it works in its similarities and differences.
Role#My role included collaborating on the design of the main premise, organizing workflow, and implimenting interactive decision making. The design and organization included creating and narrowing down ideas, as well as sketching possible story boards and interactions on a whiteboard. The implimentation included scene switching, dialogue writing, and coding in GDScript.
Reflection#Overall, this experience led be to be more confident in Godot, and introduced me to GDScript. I feel that I will use this application in the future for smaller games, and it is a great tool alongside Unity. The hackathon enviornment allowed me to try to impliment an experience through Godot without committing to a long term project. I was also excited to develop my skills in working with a team in a time crunch.
Disclaimer: AI aided in the making of the description of the game posting.
title: Red Debt Redemption

content: Watch PlaythroughView GithubCreated a game inspired by a children’s book. Selected We Are in a Book! by Mo Willams to create a classic platformer in Unity with two player characters, Elephant and Piggy. Contributed to level design, art, and part of the programming for this group project.
title: We Are In A Game

content: PlayThe theme of this Game Jam was “public” with a two day time limit. Made a “public garden” farming game in Unity that showcased plants and elements from different cultures. Ranked 4th in Jammer’s Choice. Responsible for the game design and art.
title: Garden of Unity

content: PlayExplored combining a 3D environment with 2D assets on Unity. Presented the design document, then made the game within the week of the session. Responsible for the art as well as assisting in the design of the game.
title: Evan's Home

content: PlayCreated this game in Unity about a crow collecting treasures with a group of four others. Worked as a team to design and organize this project within a week. Collaborated in designing and programming.
title: Crow Game
